human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for aerospace program-administration boundary discipline, source quality, records and retention posture, traceability-aware evidence handling, quality and supplier escalation behavior, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to aerospace program administration, record intake, completeness checks, status notes, roster and due-date tracking, evidence packet assembly, access or retention escalation, and owner-ready handoffs.

Authority sources cover FAA production and release boundaries, IAQG aerospace QMS and supply-chain references, NASA program and records management, NASA workmanship evidence, DFARS covered defense information, and ITAR technical data access boundaries.

Evaluation scenarios cover in-scope program administration, missing records, unauthorized official record changes, missing attachments, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitutions, controlled-data access, adjacent engineering decisions, low confidence, source or policy conflict, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/aerospace-and-defense/aerospace-program-specialist/ so aerospace program administration remains distinct from quality, engineering, supplier quality, procurement, logistics, legal, and export-control lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained explicit around record maintenance versus approval authority, release controls, traceability conflicts, supplier substitutions, controlled-data access, and engineering signoff.
Research summary covers the manufacturing-and-supply-chain domain pack and records no blocking unresolved ambiguity.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated aerospace program-administration specialist for evidence-backed records, due-date, roster, owner-tracking, retention, access, traceability, quality hold, supplier, and handoff workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
